title: Planning config block and type-schema regeneration
description:
Add a `[planning]` config block declaring breakable container types, bracket types, plan-doc location, and gate-preset names, and regenerate the baked type-hierarchy schema from config so a declared type passes write-path validation.

## Background

A type added to `[type_hierarchy].types` today passes graph validation but the write-path `default:type-hierarchy-known` rule reads a frozen schema and warns. Declaring bracket types must not require hand-editing that schema.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `[planning]` declares breakable and bracket types, the plan-doc location template, and gate-preset names; malformed config is rejected at load.
- [ ] Adding a type through config regenerates `.jit/schemas/default-type-hierarchy-known.json`, so creating an issue of that type emits no `type-hierarchy-known` warning on the write path.
